seo_title: How Sojun Park's Fellowship at MIT CIS Fosters International Trade…
meta_description: Discover how Sojun Park's fellowship at MIT Center for International Studies (CIS) enables international trade research and community building.
body_html:
Sojun Park's fellowship at MIT CIS provides a scholarly home for international trade research.

Building Community through Research

Park's time at CIS has been both grounding and transformative, offering him a scholarly home that has shaped his research and helped broaden his intellectual horizons. He is free to use his time toward his own research, and has found value in pursuing topics that are of interest to the CIS community — whether it’s AI or global governance.

Interdisciplinary Research Environment

Park knew that he could really benefit from the interdisciplinary environment at MIT, and specifically at CIS, where faculty are coming not only from political science, but also affiliated with the Department of Economics and MIT Sloan [School of Management]. He has published prolifically along the way, including two articles in the Review of International Organizations and the Review of International Political Economy.

Park is working on his forthcoming research project, which explores the security implications of AI technologies. His work has been influenced by CIS faculty who have previously done research on international trade and security.

The paid fellowship offers an academic year at MIT and dedicated office space at CIS, providing Park with the resources he needs to pursue his research. The fellowship has enabled Park to think through the security implications of his intellectual property research, which he may not have been able to do otherwise.

source.title: Advancing international trade research and finding community
source.summary: Sojun Park, a postdoc at the Center for International Studies, has learned much from his research on intellectual property as well as his interactions with students and mentors at MIT.
